mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month but S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% indicates labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 5% and S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a broad demand contraction, which depresses earnings and equity prices over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If joint decline is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of events over a 5-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 4.5% and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) is rising, the central bank is likely to continue tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 40% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) indicates broad economic expansion, lifting corporate profits and supporting higher S&P 500 levels over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 increases quarter-over-quarter but S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 200 signals accelerating inflation, prompting monetary tightening fears that drive short-term negative S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 and S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of such events over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in payroll employment (PAYEMS) coupled with a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms labor market weakness, which reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 35% of instances over any 4-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1800 thousand units indicate a robust housing sector, which supports consumer wealth and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the short term.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1800 and S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such events over a 5-year window, reject.
